outcome.summary: Archive this phase as a bounded success: codexhx is useful as a haxe.rust production pressure test and may continue toward named fixture-backed adapter slices, but broad Codex/Cafex replacement is deferred.

reusableArtifacts.reuse: Keep as upstream-shaped fixture gates for helper/headless work.
Keep as fail-closed host-effect and diagnostics proof surfaces.
Use as selected adapter-slice evidence; do not treat as broad replacement proof.
Keep as the evidence bundle for HXCX-6/HXCX-7 and future replacement review.
Keep JSONL/plain-file state for fixture evidence; require SQLite/sqlx or equivalent persistence before persistent state replacement.
Keep registry lookup fixtures; require real MCP transport/resource/OAuth/execution gates before MCP parity claims.
Use as the conversion map from codexhx pressure findings to generic haxe.rust issues and fixtures; remaining gaps have expected-failure repros in ../haxe.rust.

abandonedOrDeferredPaths.reason: G6 rejected broad replacement because Cafex has 7 unsupported seams, contract subset has 5 gaps, and distribution/licensing is unresolved.
Operator runbook and G6 decision keep production routing disabled until a later decision record and rollback drill.
Live TUI, credentialed model, queue, plan checkpoint, and restart ownership are unsupported seam rows.
haxe.rust GPL-3.0 with Codex/Cafex Apache-2.0 artifacts requires license review before release.
Compiler fixes must remain generic haxe.rust work; current pressure fixes were upstreamed generically.

resolvedFollowUpBeads.purpose: Created upstreamable haxe.rust expected-failure fixtures/issues for nullable interface values and stdlib path/string workarounds.
Wrote the post-experiment decision record and reusable artifact archive.
Recorded the JSONL versus SQLite state backend decision.
Recorded the MCP/tool registry skeleton and replacement gate.

brewConversionNotes.note: Convert reusable Cafex adapter harness outputs into Brew/Cafetera acceptance evidence only per named slice.
Remaining compiler work now has generic haxe.rust expected-failure fixtures; keep codexhx dependencies behind local workarounds until the linked haxe.rust beads close.
Any Cafetera workflow wiring should consume operator-runbook disable/rollback rules and must not change defaults without a new decision record.
Keep JSONL/plain files for fixture evidence; add SQLite/sqlx or equivalent persistence proof before persistent goal/runtime/app-server replacement claims.
Keep registry lookup fixtures; add real MCP transport, resource, OAuth, and execution gates before MCP parity claims.
Preserve docs, reference ledgers, fixtures, and harnesses; do not preserve generated Rust as source.
